India stands as a reliable ally, boasting a vast talent reservoir and substantial market demand. Additionally, a favorable trade deal enhances access to the US marketplace.

items.title: Businesses can digitally record 'goods delivered' on e-way bill portal: GSTN
items.summary: GST registered businesses can now digitally close e-way bills upon delivery completion. This new voluntary feature on the GST portal strengthens transaction trails and accountability. Previously, e-way bills tracked goods movement but lacked a formal completion record. This move aims to improve data integrity and traceability. Experts suggest this could become mandatory, enhancing reconciliation and curbing malpractices.

items.title: Wes Streeting pledges 'wealth tax that works'
items.summary: Wes Streeting is proposing reforms to capital gains tax, as part of his pitch for the Labour leadership.

items.title: QSR chains brace for slower growth, margin pressure after weak FY26 demand
items.summary: QSR chains are shifting focus from rapid expansion to profitability, emphasizing value meals and cost management in response to changing consumer spending habits.

items.title: Nvidia's latest record earnings fail to impress investors
items.summary: The chip giant reported more stellar results but its shares fell after-hours as investors wonder if it can keep up its pace of growth amid greater competition.

items.title: Amazon India targets beauty boom with 100 global brands as online rivalry rises
items.summary: The premium beauty segment is growing rapidly, with significant demand from tier-two and tier-three cities, and rising consumer spending projected to make India's beauty market worth $40 billion by 2030.

items.title: EasyJet boss says summer flights won't be hit by jet fuel shortages
items.summary: The airline has seen no problems with fuel, but says people are booking later because of uncertainty caused by the Iran war.

items.title: PFRDA sets up panel to look at adding more asset classes for better returns
items.summary: PFRDA is exploring new asset classes to boost long-term, stable returns for National Pension System subscribers, aiming to avoid market volatility. The regulator is also considering a Minimum Assured Pension plan and a Unified Pension Scheme for the private sector, while actively engaging farmers, MSMEs, and SHG groups to promote retirement savings.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Sensex, Nifty settle marginally lower despite positive start

items.title: Odisha NBFC Annapurna Finance to raise $75-100 mn to facilitate exits for early backers
items.summary: The microlender's last equity fundraise was more than two years ago when Piramal Alternatives Trust, a wholly owned subsidiary of Piramal Enterprises, acquired a 10.4% stake in Annapurna for ₹300 crore.

items.title: Lenskart shares soar despite Q4 profit decline on strong growth outlook
items.summary: The stock closed at ₹498.50, after hitting an intraday high of ₹517, compared to the previous close of ₹486.85

items.title: Grasim Industries’ stock zooms 6% as Q4 profit jump 28%
items.summary: Grasim Industries reported a 28 per cent y-o-y rise in consolidated net profit for the March quarter at ₹3,802 crore.

items.title: R K Swamy bets on integrated advertising model as global agency giants split media and creative
items.summary: In a tough financial year where big advertising networks underwent layoffs and AI-led disruption, R K Swamy posted a 15% jump in annual revenue in FY26 and a 30% jump in profits excluding tax and exceptional items.

items.title: RBI rate hikes to start in June, says Standard Chartered
items.summary: RBI rate hike: Economists at Standard Chartered anticipate India's central bank will begin raising interest rates as early as June due to escalating inflation risks from higher crude prices. They predict 50 basis points of hikes, split between June and August, to anchor sentiment and manage second-order effects on the rupee and inflation.

items.title: US team may visit India next month for trade talks: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: American trade officials are expected in India next month for crucial trade discussions. This visit follows recent US investments exceeding sixty billion dollars. India and the US are strengthening their partnership in technology, defense, and energy. Both nations aim to build resilient supply chains. Indian businesses are encouraged to support MSMEs with faster payments, boosting economic growth.

items.title: IPL Biologicals opens 3rd plant in Vadodara, raises capacity to 7,000 KL/year
items.summary: IPL Biologicals Ltd has inaugurated its third biological manufacturing facility in Vadodara, Gujarat. This new plant represents a Rs 200 crore investment, boosting the company's annual production capacity to 7,000 kilolitres. The facility will produce biofertilisers, biopesticides, and crop health products. This expansion aligns with government goals to reduce chemical fertiliser use. The company plans further investment for future phases.

items.title: Union Minister Nitin Gadkari urges joint ventures with US firms to harness advanced technologies
items.summary: Union Minister Nitin Gadkari has urged Indian companies to form joint ventures with US firms to access advanced technologies and boost innovation. He said the Road Transport Ministry is exploring US consultancy support for road project reports, while highlighting the sharp decline in India’s logistics costs. Emphasizing clean energy adoption and sustainable mobility, Gadkari expressed confidence that India can emerge as the world’s leading automobile industry within the next five years.

items.title: UK agrees £3.7bn trade deal with six Gulf states
items.summary: The deal will remove an estimated £580m worth of tariffs from British exports, but rights groups are critical

items.title: India considering several measures to stop CAD from widening further, says Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India CAD: India is watching the fluctuations in the global economy as policymakers deliberate on strategies to address the growing current account deficit, Piyush Goyal said. With the rupee under pressure amid uncertain global trade dynamics, the latest figures show a worrying trend for the December quarter; Nonetheless, the fiscal year's total deficit remains manageable, the minister added.

items.title: SpiceJet faces fresh legal heat as engine lessor seeks enforcement of $8 million UK award
items.summary: Sunbird France moved the Delhi High Court to enforce a London court ruling over unpaid engine lease rentals and maintenance dues, adding to SpiceJet’s mounting legal and financial troubles.

items.title: Elon Musk hiring engineers for SpaceXAI, says no prior AI experience needed: ‘Smart humans figure it out fast’
items.summary: SpaceX CEO Elon Musk has launched a hiring push for “world-class engineers/physicists” for SpaceXAI, saying applicants do not need prior AI experience. The move signals SpaceX’s growing focus on artificial intelligence across rockets, Starlink and future autonomous systems.

items.title: AMD invests $10 Billion in Taiwan to build AI chips and challenge Nvidia's dominance
items.summary: Advanced Micro Devices has pledged more than $10 billion in Taiwan to expand AI chip production with partners including TSMC and ASE Technology, as the chipmaker intensifies its challenge to Nvidia's dominance in artificial intelligence computing.

items.title: TikTok and YouTube 'not safe enough' for kids, says Ofcom
items.summary: YouTube said it worked with experts to provide appropriate experiences. TikTok said it was disappointed Ofcom had not acknowledged its safety features.

items.title: FMCG products set to cost more if global pressures persist, hurting consumption
items.summary: Experts said Indian FMCG products may become costlier due to reduced raw material stocks and rising inflation linked to the Iran-US conflict. Significant price hikes in dairy and staples reflect broader inflationary pressures, while household consumption growth may slow amid economic uncertainty.

items.title: India weighs rate hike to steady the rupee
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is considering all of its available options to stabilize the rupee, including an interest rate hike, more currency swaps and raising dollars from investors overseas, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: Indian Consulate to host webinar to help exporters sell their goods in US
items.summary: Indian exporters can learn to sell products in the US through a webinar by the Indian Consulate in New York and Walmart. This event will detail supply chain and compliance needs for the American market. It follows a similar successful webinar with Costco. A new trade portal also aims to boost India-US business and unlock opportunities for Indian businesses.

items.title: India factory growth likely slowed in May as manufacturing loses momentum amid Iran war, services stay resilient
items.summary: Early PMI data shows manufacturing easing to a multi-month low while services continue to support overall private-sector expansion.

items.title: Japan’s megabanks post record profits, but analysts warn growth may slow as risks mount
items.summary: Japan's largest lenders may face some challenges to keep profit levels at record highs, amid higher credit costs and current geopolitical risks

items.title: India needs to raise food grains production to 450 million tonnes by 2047: Chaudhari
items.summary: Director General SK Chaudhari emphasized that intelligent technology use in agriculture and the fertilizer sector is a national imperative. He highlighted blockchain and AI as key tools for transforming the sector.

items.title: Elon Musk's X fined for not complying with Australia's child protection laws
items.summary: The social media giant will pay A$650,000 plus legal costs, ending a three-year legal saga.

items.title: Samsung strike on hold as workers push for AI bonus
items.summary: The walkout, which was due to start on Thursday, has been suspended while union members vote on a tentative deal.

items.title: NCLAT says NCLT cannot directly order SFIO Probe; refers matter to Centre
items.summary: The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) has held that the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) could not directly order an investigation by the Serious Fraud Investigation Office (SFIO) and clarified that such power rests with the Central Government under the Companies Act.

items.title: Scapia raises $63 million led by General Catalyst to scale AI-led travel fintech play
items.summary: Travel fintech startup Scapia has raised $63 million in a Series C round led by General Catalyst to scale AI-led products, expand partnerships and grow its Gen Z user base.

items.title: K Hospitality launches investment arm with ₹200 cr corpus to back consumer companies
items.summary: K Hospitality Corp has launched Kliff Ventures with a ₹200 crore corpus to invest in early-stage consumer and retail brands across food, lifestyle and allied sectors.

items.title: Iran war dampens India private sector growth, services pick up, PMI shows
items.summary: India's private sector growth slowed in May. Manufacturing faced a slowdown, impacting new orders and production. Services saw a marginal increase in activity. International demand weakened due to global conflicts. Input costs rose, but firms absorbed some of these costs. Job creation was stronger in services. Business optimism declined, indicating challenges ahead.

items.title: Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan Chase will hire more AI specialists, fewer traditional bankers
items.summary: Jamie Dimon said JPMorgan will increasingly hire AI specialists and fewer traditional bankers as artificial intelligence reshapes the financial sector. The bank is expanding investments in AI across operations, joining a wider Wall Street trend of prioritising tech talent and automation.

items.title: Parle Melody: The ₹1 soft power twist Modi served to Meloni and the world
items.summary: Prime Minister Modi's gift of a Parle Melody toffee to Italy's Giorgia Meloni symbolised India's evolving soft power. Beyond traditional exports, this gesture highlights India's growing confidence in its mass-market culture and everyday consumer identity, signaling a shift towards showcasing its vibrant domestic economy on the global stage.

items.title: India-Nordic Summit signals New Delhi's emergence as 'co-author of global governance': Analysis
items.summary: When Denmark's Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen described India in Oslo as "not a middle power, but one of the world's greatest powers," it signalled more than diplomatic warmth. It reflected a broader geopolitical transition in which India is increasingly being seen as a co-author of global climate, technology and governance frameworks.

items.title: RBI mulls all options, including rate hike, as rupee slumps
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is exploring all avenues to steady the rupee. Options include raising interest rates and currency swaps. Officials are meeting to discuss actions after the rupee hit a new low. Measures mirror those from 2013. The central bank aims to stop depreciation and is ready to act.

items.title: Oil prices trade marginally higher as traders await clarity on peace deal, Trump says 'no hurry' on deal with Iran
items.summary: Oil prices rose marginally on Thursday after a steep selloff, as traders weighed uncertain Iran-US negotiations and continued disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: US seeks to boost energy exports to India as Rubio visits Delhi
items.summary: The Trump administration is aiming to increase energy exports to India, with Ambassador Sergio Gor highlighting India's receptiveness to diversifying its energy sources. This comes as India faces an energy crisis due to the ongoing war with Iran, which has disrupted vital shipping lanes. Secretary of State Marco Rubio's upcoming visit will focus on energy security, trade, and defense cooperation.

items.title: US Envoy says ‘big things ahead’ in India-US nuclear energy collaboration
items.summary: Big things are coming for India-US nuclear energy cooperation. US Ambassador Sergio Gor sees major collaboration ahead. Maharashtra's Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis highlighted the state's readiness to lead nuclear energy transformation. American companies are invited to invest in Maharashtra. The US also looks forward to celebrating its 250th birthday with India, its democratic partner.

items.title: From Saudi Aramco to Alibaba: 7 biggest IPOs before Elon Musk’s SpaceX market debut
items.summary: SpaceX could become the biggest IPO in history, but before Elon Musk’s space giant hits public markets, companies like Saudi Aramco, Alibaba, Visa and Meta dominated global listings. Here’s a look at the seven largest IPOs ever by funds raised.

items.title: Mark Zuckerberg 'feeling weight of' mass layoffs at Meta, says ‘success isn’t a given’ in memo
items.summary: Mark Zuckerberg sent a formal email to his staff ahead of the layoffs, addressing it and thanking those who have been sacked for their work.

items.title: 'India, Italy signed critical MoUs in various sectors,' says MEA Secy (West)

Both nations aim to boost bilateral trade to €20 billion by 2029 and are committed to early implementation of IMEC, fostering deeper cooperation across various sectors.

items.title: Marriott bets on outbound Indian travel surge, rewires loyalty into experiences
items.summary: Marriott International is focusing on the Indian market as travel demand grows, especially among younger travellers. The company is transforming its loyalty programs to prioritize experiential rewards and has formed partnerships with various sectors to enhance customer engagement.

items.title: Microfinance sector recovers after stress cycle but caution prevails amid geopolitical, inflation risks
items.summary: India's microfinance institutions are cautiously recovering after two years of stress, with improved collection efficiency and asset quality. Analysts warn that while conditions are stabilizing, growth will be disciplined due to geopolitical risks and a changing lending environment.

items.title: Tata Capital listing cushions Tata Sons in a difficult FY26
items.summary: The one-off boost from Tata Capital's listing comes even as Tata Trusts Chairman Noel Tata raises concern over mounting losses in Tata Digital to Air India.

items.title: The fight against foreign developers buying Caribbean beaches

items.title: CCI to onboard analysts and data scientists
items.summary: The Competition Commission of India is enhancing its capabilities to understand digital markets and artificial intelligence. The regulator is hiring data scientists and analysts with expertise in AI tools and digital market operations. This move aims to strengthen the CCI's oversight. The CCI is also increasing approvals for non-problematic transactions through an automated route.

items.title: Supermarkets hit back over pressure to cap price of milk, bread and eggs
items.summary: A minister confirms talks have taken place but says there will be no mandatory cap on essential food prices.

items.title: Melody to ears? India's toffee exports are on a roll
items.summary: Exports of toffees and similar confectionery products grew over 10% in volume and 166% in rupee terms since FY14, reaching as many as 74 countries during the period.

items.title: India’s core sector growth edges up to 1.7% in April on steel, cement boost
items.summary: Steel production rose 6.2% year-on-year in April, cement output increased 9.4%, while electricity generation expanded 4.1%, supporting the overall index growth.

items.title: UK waters down new Russian oil sanctions as fuel prices rise
items.summary: The change reflects increasing supply concerns over certain fuels due to the effective blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Fuel duty freeze extended until the end of the year
items.summary: Fuel duty was initially cut by 5p in March 2022, under the Conservative government.

items.title: College career path ‘over’ as skilled trade get 30% pay bump, recruitment giant says
items.summary: "I would say you can make a good career and good money in skilled trade. That's definitely a career track," CEO of world's largest recruitment firm told CNBC.

items.title: UN agency flags India growth slowdown to 6.4% in FY27 as Iran war fuels global inflation, cost pressures
items.summary: A UN report has warned of weaker global growth, rising inflation and tighter financial conditions, even as India remains among the fastest-growing major economies with a gradual recovery expected in FY28.

items.title: Inflation falls to 2.8% but is expected to rise from here
items.summary: Energy prices were lower due to the government's energy bill support package and lower wholesale prices before the Iran war.

items.title: Only 8% of Atal Pension Yojana’s subscribers opt for ₹5,000 monthly slab; 87% choose minimum sum of ₹1,000
items.summary: The Atal Pension Yojana, launched in 2015, has over 90 million enrolments, with significant participation from younger age groups. The persistency rate stood at 50.1% as of 31 March.

items.title: TV presenter says abusive ex gave her no access to her own money
items.summary: Ruth Dodsworth's former husband was jailed for coercive and controlling behaviour and stalking.
